title: Upper Crust Irish Cod Gratin
description: A sour, flaky, cozy Irish dish featuring soda water and a grill, making it gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Rinse the cod fillets and pat dry with paper towels.
In a bowl, mix together flour and a pinch of salt.
Dredge the cod fillets in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Place the cod fillets on the grill and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the cod is cooking, mix the melted butter, soda water, and sour cream (if using) in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook until slightly thickened.
Stir in the shredded cheddar cheese until melted and smooth.
Transfer the cooked cod to a baking dish and top with the cheese sauce.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and golden brown.
